name: GWS Calendar Integration
description: Google Calendar: Manage calendars and events using the Google Workspace gws CLI. Covers events, calendars, ACL rules, free/busy queries, calendar lists, and settings.

covenant_rules: Prerequisite: gws-shared must be loaded first for authentication and global flags.
Always confirm with the user before creating, updating, deleting, or moving calendar events.
Respect calendar visibility rules — never expose private event details from secondary calendars without explicit user intent.
Use gws schema calendar.<resource>.<method> to inspect required parameters before constructing API calls.

triad_templates.left_hemisphere: Evaluate the Calendar API method, timezone handling, recurrence rules, and attendee data for: {query}. Verify the operation respects calendar access controls.
triad_templates.right_hemisphere: Design the exact gws calendar command with proper resource, method, --params, --json for event bodies, --format flags, and timeMin/timeMax filters for: {query}.
triad_templates.main_hemisphere: Coordinate Calendar operations. Inspect method schema, construct command, confirm event mutations, execute, and present schedule results: {query}.

steps.name: Inspect Calendar API Method Schema
steps.action: Use 'gws schema calendar.<resource>.<method>' to inspect required parameters for the target Calendar API call. Resources: acl, calendarList, calendars, channels, colors, events, freebusy, settings.

steps.name: Construct Calendar Command
steps.action: Build the full 'gws calendar <resource> <method>' command with --params for filters (timeMin, timeMax, q, calendarId), --json for event bodies (summary, description, start, end, attendees, recurrence), and --format for display.

steps.name: Confirm Calendar Mutations
steps.action: For create, update, delete, move, import, or quickAdd operations, present the command details to the user for confirmation. For read-only operations (get, list, instances, freebusy query), proceed automatically.

steps.name: Execute Calendar Command
steps.action: Execute the gws calendar command in the terminal. For events.list include timeMin, timeMax, or q parameter for targeted queries. Use --page-all for large result sets.

steps.name: Format and Present Calendar Results
steps.action: Parse the JSON output and present events in a readable table: date/time, event title, calendar, attendees. For free/busy queries show time blocks. For event details show full description and attendees.

steps.name: Calendar Operation Completed
steps.action: Confirm the Calendar operation result. Provide next-step suggestions such as creating an event from a free slot, modifying recurrence, or sharing a calendar.
